zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret of Romania於1382年出生在羅馬尼亞時，她的父親The Exiled Emperor Constantine II Asen of Bulgaria13歲，母親Irene Palaiologina Philanthropene133歲。她於1395年與Sir Alexander Lound在英國英格蘭越可爾約克結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾韋斯特 巴特維其可。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Romania
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Czech and Slovak (Romaník): from a pet form of the personal name Roman .

variants.name: Roman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Angel, Jorge, Miguel, Pedro, Francisco, Julio, Mario.

Spanish and Hungarian (Román); Catalan, French, English, Dutch, German, Romanian, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Ukrainian, Rusyn, Belorussian, and Croatian: from the Latin personal name Romanus, which originally meant ‘Roman’. This name was borne by several Christian saints, including a 7th-century bishop of Rouen, and was a baptismal name of the Russian prince Boris , who is venerated as a saint in the Orthodox Church. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of Slavic patronymics like Polish Romanowicz . Compare Romano .
Americanized form of German Romann: variant of 1 above or from the ancient Germanic personal name Hrodman (see Rodeman ).

variants.name: Romanick
meanings: Americanized form of Czech and Slovak Romaník (see Romanik ).
Americanized form of Serbian and Croatian Romanić (see Romanic ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret of Romania was born in 1382, in Romania, her father, The Exiled Emperor Constantine II Asen of Bulgaria, was 13 and her mother, Irene Palaiologina Philanthropene, was 133. She married Sir Alexander Lound in 1395, in York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in West Butterwick, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret of Romania est née en 1382, à Roumanie, son père, The Exiled Emperor Constantine II Asen of Bulgaria, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Irene Palaiologina Philanthropene, avait 133 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Alexander Lound en 1395, à York, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à West Butterwick, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret of Romaniaが1382年にルーマニアで生まれた時，父The Exiled Emperor Constantine II Asen of Bulgariaは13歳，母Irene Palaiologina Philanthropeneは133歳であった。1395年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアヨークでSir Alexander Loundと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウエスト ブッテルウィックで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret of Romania(이)가 1382년 루마니아에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 The Exiled Emperor Constantine II Asen of Bulgaria(은)는 13세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Irene Palaiologina Philanthropene(은)는 133세였다. 그녀는 1395년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 요르크에서 Sir Alexander Lound(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 웨스트 버터윅에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret of Romania родилась в 1382 г. в местности Румыния, её отцу по имени The Exiled Emperor Constantine II Asen of Bulgaria было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Irene Palaiologina Philanthropene, 133. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Alexander Lound в 1395 г. в местности ерк, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности вест буттирвикк, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.
